title: carex divulsa subsp. chabertii
intro: Carex divulsa subsp. chabertii, also known as Chabert's sedge, is a subspecies of grass-like perennial plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with roots and rhizomes containing compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Carex divulsa subsp. chabertii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of this plant can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
